content.problemStatement:
Let us define the score of an array BBB (B1,B2,B3,...,Bk)(B_1, B_2, B_3,...,B_k)(B1‚Äč,B2‚Äč,B3‚Äč,...,Bk‚Äč) as:

score(B)={‚ąĎi=1kBi,if k>1 and Max(B)‚ąíSecondMax(B)<X.‚ąí‚ąĎi=1kBi,otherwise.\begin{equation}
  score(B)=\begin{cases}
    \sum_{i=1}^{k}B_i, & \text{if } k \gt 1 \text{ and } Max(B) - SecondMax(B) \lt X.\\
    -\sum_{i=1}^{k}B_i, & \text{otherwise}.
  \end{cases}
\end{equation}score(B)={‚ąĎi=1k‚ÄčBi‚Äč,‚ąí‚ąĎi=1k‚ÄčBi‚Äč,‚Äčif k>1 and Max(B)‚ąíSecondMax(B)<X.otherwise.‚Äč‚Äč‚ÄčHere, SecondMax(B)SecondMax(B)SecondMax(B) denotes the second element in BBB if we sort it in non-increasing order.

You are given an array AAA with NNN elements. You can partition it into multiple continuous subarrays, aiming to **maximize** the **sum of the scores** of each of the subarrays. Note that, in a partition, each element of the array belongs to **exactly** one subarray.

Additionally, you should perform QQQ queries. For each given value of XXX, find the **maximum sum of score** if you partition the array optimally.


Input Format

The first line contains TTT - the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first line of each test case contains two integers NNN and QQQ - the size of the array AAA and the number of queries.
The second line contains NNN space-separated integers A1,A2,‚Ä¶,ANA_1, A_2, \ldots, A_NA1‚Äč,A2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,AN‚Äč.
The third line contains QQQ space-separated integers X1,X2,‚Ä¶,XQX_1, X_2, \ldots, X_QX1‚Äč,X2‚Äč,‚Ä¶,XQ‚Äč.


Output Format
For each test case, output QQQ space-separated integers in a new line, the maximum sum of score you can obtain.
constraints: Sum of NNN over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.
Sum of QQQ over all test cases does not exceed 3‚čÖ1053\cdot 10^53‚čÖ105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 111: In the third query of the first testcase, an optimal partition is [1,4,3],[6,2][1, 4, 3], [6, 2][1,4,3],[6,2]. The sum of score is (1+4+3)‚ąí(6+2)=0(1 + 4 + 3) - (6 + 2) = 0(1+4+3)‚ąí(6+2)=0.
Test case 222: In the second query of the second testcase, an optimal partition is [5,7],[10],[1,1][5, 7], [10], [1, 1][5,7],[10],[1,1]. The sum of score is (5+7)‚ąí(10)+(1+1)=4(5 + 7) - (10) + (1 + 1) = 4(5+7)‚ąí(10)+(1+1)=4.
